3.1.rationale: Implementing the proposed multidisciplinary team (10.1), defining fairness and accessibility standards (5.1), and conducting accessibility reviews (10.2) and acceptance testing (6.4) will proactively identify and resolve parsing issues that strip accessibility features. 3.2.rationale: The system is a deterministic parser with no decision-making capabilities. With the proposed acceptance testing (6.4) and accessibility reviews (10.2) in place, even theoretical parsing errors affecting specific language scripts will be caught prior to deployment, reducing the likelihood to Rare. The consequence remains Insignificant.

3.3.rationale: As a deterministic parser, the tool does not generate new content. The consequence remains Insignificant.

3.4.rationale: The implementation of standard operating procedures for human intervention (6.7), combined with acceptance testing (6.4), a pilot phase (6.5), and production monitoring (6.6), will catch and mitigate formatting corruption or parsing errors before they cause operational harm. 3.5.rationale: The core ephemeral architecture minimizes data retention risks. This reduces the likelihood to Unlikely, while the consequence remains Moderate.

3.6.rationale: The proposed network-level egress controls, strict input validation (7.3), and ephemeral processing architecture, once validated through the recommended security testing (6.4), will secure cross-agency data handling and prevent unauthorized access to classified documents. This reduces the likelihood of reputational damage to Unlikely, with the consequence remaining Minor.
